title: Bridge Essay: Reductions in perceived stress following Transcendental Meditation practice are associated with increased brain regional connectivity at rest.
concept: Mantra — inner speech, focused attention meditation, chanting neural effects.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Language/Mantra** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Mantra — inner speech, focused attention meditation, chanting neural effects.

**Reductions in perceived stress following Transcendental Meditation practice are associated with increased brain regional connectivity at rest.** - Brain Cogn (2020). DOI: 10.1016/j.bandc.2020.105517. PMID: 31945602. Transcendental Meditation (TM) is defined as a mental process of transcending using a silent mantra. Previous work showed that relatively brief period of TM practice leads to decreases in stress and anxiety. However, whether these changes are subserved by specific morpho-functional brain modifications (as observed in other meditation techniques) is still unclear.
